name: Western Union Money Order Only
description_short: Western Union offers in-person money transfers, money orders, bill payments, and cash pickup services at hundreds of agent locations across the US and internationally.
description_long:
Western Union has operated as a trusted money transfer service since 1851, establishing itself as a reliable provider of in-person financial services. The company operates through hundreds of agent locations across the United States, including major retail partners like Walmart and grocery chains, making their services widely accessible to consumers.

Western Union's core offerings include domestic and international money transfers conducted in person at agent locations, money order purchase and cashing services, bill payment options (utilities, rent, car payments), cash pickup for funds sent by others, and prepaid Visa card receipt options. Customers can initiate transfers online or through the Western Union app and complete payment in cash at participating locations, or conduct the entire transaction in person with agent assistance.

The company distinguishes itself through its extensive physical network, long operational history, multiple payment methods (cash, debit, and select credit card options), and dual online-to-in-person workflow that allows customers to save time. Western Union also provides Money Transfer Control Numbers (MTCNs) for tracking transfers and offers currency exchange services for international transactions at the time of transfer.

However, prospective users should note that Western Union does not provide in-person currency exchange services, pricing varies significantly by transfer method and destination, and service hours depend on individual agent location host businesses. The company is experiencing processing delays for specific payment corridors (Orange Wallet in Cameroon at time of website capture), and customers must have valid identification and recipient information to complete transactions.
address: 321 W Indian School Rd, Phoenix, AZ 85013

company_info.headquarters: 321 W Indian School Rd, Phoenix, AZ 85013

pros: Extensive network of 400+ agent locations across US including Walmart and major grocery chains for convenient access
Established 173-year operational history (since 1851) providing long track record of reliability
Hybrid online-to-in-person service model allows customers to register transfers online and pay in cash at locations to save counter time
Multiple payment options at participating locations: cash, debit card, and select credit card acceptance
Money Transfer Control Number (MTCN) tracking system enables both sender and recipient to confirm transaction status
Supports multiple service types at single locations: money transfers, bill payments, money orders, and cash pickup
Money orders explicitly marketed as trusted alternative to checks for sending cash securely
cons: No in-person currency exchange services available—international recipients receive funds in local currency based on rates determined at time of transfer, not in advance
Pricing varies significantly by transfer method, payment option, and destination with no single transparent fee structure; calculator tool required to compare costs
Subject to processing delays on specific payment corridors (website documents current delays for Orange Wallet payments to Cameroon)
Service hours entirely dependent on host business locations (retail partners, banks), requiring individual location lookups rather than consistent hours
Requires valid government-issued ID and complete recipient information (name as appears on ID, location) which may be barrier for some users
best_for: Consumers sending cash domestically to recipients who need immediate in-person pickup without bank account
Individuals paying bills (utilities, rent, car payments) in cash at convenient retail locations near their home or work
Senders conducting international transfers who want established service with 173-year operational history and wide geographic reach
Consumers preferring to avoid checks and using money orders as secure alternative for sending cash through mail or in person
diagnosis: Western Union is best for consumers who need to send or receive cash in person at convenient retail locations, pay bills without bank accounts, or conduct international transfers through an established service. The main caveat is that prices and service hours vary significantly by location and transfer type, requiring upfront research using their cost calculator and location finder tools before completing transactions.
services: Domestic in-person money transfers at agent locations
International in-person money transfers with currency conversion at time of transfer
Money order purchase at participating locations
Money order cashing at participating locations
Bill payment services (utilities, rent, car payments) at agent locations
Cash pickup for funds sent via Western Union MTCN
Online transfer initiation with in-person payment completion
Prepaid Visa Card receipt option for money transfers
Money Transfer Control Number (MTCN) tracking system
Service availability at bank partner locations
Service availability at retail locations including Walmart and grocery chains
